meaning: n.提示,付款期限vt.提示,鼓动,促使,(给演员)提白adj.敏捷的,迅速的,即时的adv.准时地
examples: The meeting will begin at ten o'clock prompt.
You have been so prompt in carrying out all these commissions.
There are many emergencies which need prompt first aid treatment.
But all these deals would prompt antitrust scrutiny, and no final resolution is expected anytime soon.
There were concerns that a criticism from G20 may prompt Japan to alter its aggressive stance.
